summary: The group's dark horse. Under Gustavo Alfaro, Paraguay conceded just 10 goals in 18 South American qualifiers and lost only once in his 12 competitive games — beating Brazil, Uruguay and defending champions Argentina along the way. A stubborn, defence-first side that was also the most direct team in South America.
style: Defence-first 4-4-2 mid-block — intense, physical, shuffling side-to-side to block central passes, then snapping into an aggressive press once the ball crosses halfway. When they win it, they break fast: the most direct team in CONMEBOL qualifying — few passes, ball moved rapidly to hit the spaces. 'Centro, cabeza y gol' — cross, header, goal.

counterReliance.note: Stubborn mid-block, then the most direct counters in South America

strengths: Elite defensive organisation — only 10 conceded in 18 qualifiers; beat Argentina, Brazil and Uruguay
Most tackles and 2nd-most attacking-third recoveries in CONMEBOL qualifying — can press high as well as soak
The most direct attack in South America — rapid counters and crossing for the aerial threat
weaknesses: Lowest-ranked side in the group; cedes possession by design and relies on its moments

keyPlayers.name: Julio Enciso
keyPlayers.note: The potential match-winner — runs at opponents with a neat final-third touch. At Copa América 2024 took 14 shots and 22 take-ons, both far more than any team-mate; scored the 2022-23 Premier League goal of the season

keyPlayers.name: Diego Gómez
keyPlayers.note: A ferocious right-foot striker of the ball — a long-range threat

tactics: Frustrate possession-dominant opponents in a disciplined mid-block, then counter fast and direct — the longer they stay level, the more they grow into the game. A genuine threat to upset anyone in Group D.
notes: Lowest FIFA ranking in the group (40th) but arguably its form side — unbeaten run and elite defensive record.
sources: The Athletic — 2026 World Cup tactical guide, Group D (5 Jun 2026)
John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
